reasoning: Because no search tools are available and the knowledge cutoff (October 2025) is effectively in the future relative to the model's training data, I must rely solely on base rates. Given that 2025 is widely anticipated to be part of an extended, gradual Fed easing cycle, the probability of a standard 25 basis point (bps) rate cut at any given meeting is relatively high. However, October represents a 'non-SEP' (Summary of Economic Projections) meeting, where the Fed has historically been slightly less likely to make changes unless forced by economic conditions or if cutting at every consecutive meeting. As a result, the base rate for a standard 25 bps cut at an arbitrary non-SEP meeting during an easing phase is roughly 30-40%. I estimate the probability at 35%.
